concepts.name: Conditional Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of an event occurring given that another event has already occurred.

concepts.name: Joint Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of two events occurring simultaneously.

concepts.name: Marginal Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of a single event occurring, irrespective of other events.

concepts.name: Law of Total Probability
concepts.short_definition: The probability of an event is the sum of its probabilities over all possible partitions of the sample space.

concepts.name: Bayes' Theorem
concepts.short_definition: A theorem that describes the probability of an event based on prior knowledge of conditions that might be related to the event.

concepts.name: Prior Probability
concepts.short_definition: The initial probability of an event before considering new evidence.

concepts.name: Posterior Probability
concepts.short_definition: The updated probability of an event after considering new evidence.

concepts.name: Likelihood
concepts.short_definition: The probability of observing the evidence given a specific hypothesis.

concepts.name: Evidence
concepts.short_definition: The total probability of observing the evidence, averaged over all possible hypotheses.

concepts.name: Bayesian Inference
concepts.short_definition: A statistical method that uses Bayes' Theorem to update the probability of a hypothesis as more evidence or information becomes available.
